summary: Full backend + frontend end-to-end testing for AutoAcquire AI MVP. 27/27 pytest backend cases pass covering auth, tenant isolation, public seller AI chat (Gemini 3.1 Pro live), lead scoring determinism, vehicle correction + audit, human takeover/resume, manual messages, inventory list/template/CSV import with error rows, RBAC (rep denied for rule creation & CSV import; admin denied on /platform), platform admin org listing, qualification-rules CRUD, appointments availability + booking + 409 double-book, analytics overview. Frontend Playwright covered login redirect to /app/dashboard, dashboard panels, leads list + score-band filter + navigation to detail, inventory action buttons, appointments/analytics/settings pages, and public seller chat at /sell/prestige-auto-toronto with real Gemini AI reply and localStorage conversation persistence.

issues: Demo credentials + demo password are prefilled and shown in a visible panel — expected for demo build but note for prod hardening.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 845 lines and could be split into modular routers (leads, inventory, appointments, analytics, platform) for maintainability, but is acceptable for MVP scale.

CORS_ORIGINS default '*' combined with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per CORS spec — browsers will refuse credentialed requests from arbitrary origins. Since the app uses Bearer tokens (not cookies), this doesn't break functionality today, but should be tightened to explicit origins in production.
auth.get_current_user still falls back to reading access_token cookie, but the login endpoint never sets one — dead code path; harmless but confusing.
public appointment booking endpoint (_conflict check) only guards duplicate start_time; overlapping (partial) slots at different start_times are still bookable. Acceptable for MVP.
seed.py runs on every startup — currently idempotent but the platform_admin and per-user seeding should skip re-hashing if user already exists to speed up cold starts (not verified in this run).
ai_engine on_error path returns valid=False after 2 retries; server.py already handles this with FALLBACK_MESSAGE + needs_review — good.
compute_score is fully deterministic and side-effect free (verified: two consecutive recalc-score calls returned identical scores).
test_credentials: admin@autoacquire.ai/Admin123!, manager@autoacquire.ai/Manager123!, rep1@autoacquire.ai/Rep123!, platform@autoacquire.ai/Platform123!. Public slug: prestige-auto-toronto
seed_data_creation: None created by testing agent beyond a TEST_ inventory row via CSV import test and a TEST_min_year qualification rule that is deleted at end of its test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py covers all major API contracts; keep it as regression suite. AI extraction test (test_lead_updated_from_ai_extraction) depends on live Gemini output — it currently passes because Gemini reliably extracts make/model/year from the prompt but could become flaky if provider degrades. Frontend test covered core flows only, did not deeply test Settings tabs (Profile, Users, Availability, AI Behavior, Audit Log inner content) or Platform admin org table UI — main agent can add if needed. Public seller chat successfully renders AI response with real Gemini and persists conversation across reloads via localStorage key aa_conv_<slug>.
